title: ranunculus arvensis subsp. tuberculatus
intro: Ranunculus arvensis subsp. tuberculatus, also known as the tuberous buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus arvensis plant, a type of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, the tuberous buttercup has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of ranunculin and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial effects. In herbal preparations, the tuberous buttercup is often used as a tincture, infused in oil, or dried as a tea to leverage its medicinal properties.
